districts.name: Lock Gate
districts.summary: Barges wait under lettering that explains water's obligations in exquisite detail.
districts.featureDescription: Copper numerals announce the water level in a voice borrowed from the last engineer.

districts.name: Letter Square
districts.summary: Public inscriptions, apprentices, and legal copyists make civic language physically crowded.
districts.featureDescription: One open space in the inscription waits for a letter nobody has designed safely.

districts.name: Canal Workshops
districts.summary: Gate teeth, reed pens, pumps, and bright brass parts occupy every dry surface.
districts.featureDescription: Miniature gates redirect a thumb-width canal that nonetheless has a tide.

landmarks.name: the drowned alphabet
landmarks.description: Stone letters lie beneath clear canal water; fish arrange themselves into missing punctuation.
